features.summary: Goldman Sachs reported Q2 2022 net revenues of $11.9 billion and EPS of $7.73, with ROE of 10.6%. Investment Banking revenues declined 41% year-over-year to $2.1 billion due to reduced capital markets activity, while Global Markets revenues surged 32% to $6.5 billion driven by increased client activity amid market volatility. Asset Management faced headwinds with net losses from on-balance sheet investments, though management fees grew 39%. Consumer & Wealth Management achieved record revenues of $2.2 billion, up 25% year-over-year.
features.management_outlook: Management remains committed to medium-term 2024 targets despite current market uncertainty, expressing confidence in the firm's ability to navigate volatile conditions. The company plans to manage resources cautiously and dynamically while continuing strategic investments. Goldman Sachs intends to target a 3% GSIB surcharge and raised its quarterly dividend by 25% to $2.50 per share. Management expects continued volatility and uncertainty but believes the diverse, global business model provides resilience.
features.risks: The firm faces significant macroeconomic headwinds including persistent inflation, tightening monetary policy, and geopolitical impacts from the Ukraine war. Credit provisions increased due to loan growth and deteriorating economic indicators, though portfolio fundamentals remain strong. On-balance sheet equity investments experienced substantial losses amid market declines. Capital markets activity remains severely depressed, and there's uncertainty about timing of recovery in ECM and DCM businesses.
features.key_drivers: Strong performance in Global Markets driven by increased client intermediation needs during volatile conditions, with FICC financing achieving record results. Transaction Banking continued robust growth with nearly 400 clients and $65 billion in deposits. The firm's diversified revenue streams and global client franchise provided stability during challenging market conditions. Management and other fees grew across wealth management and asset management segments, supporting the strategy to increase fee-based revenues.
